domain: LM (Memory Systems), Graph ML, Scientific ML (Agriculture)
contract_traceability: research/research_contract.yaml — defines claims, baselines, evaluation requirements
research/executive_summary.md — novelty assessment and repositioning recommendation
research/research_analysis.md — detailed landscape, gap analysis, falsification conditions
architect/architecture_blueprint.md — design decisions, inductions, ablations, risks
coder/ — full PyTorch implementation with config, data_model, kg, semantic, controller, model
baseline_status: Zep / Graphiti — NOT implemented (requires external system integration); TODO: unverified
Mem0 (Apr 2026) — NOT implemented (requires external system integration); TODO: unverified
Letta / MemGPT — NOT implemented (requires external system integration); TODO: unverified
AOI three-layer — NOT implemented (requires external system integration); TODO: unverified
Proposed - vector_only — NOT implemented (requires alternative episodic implementation); PARTIAL

grounded: Monolithic neural memory collapses under semantic density (arXiv:2601.15313) — theoretical grounding reproduced in architecture rationale
CLS bicameral design is the theoretically grounded solution — architecture implements the separation
Temporal KG enables temporal reasoning — validated via benchmark_temporal_reasoning
GCN encoder with edge features mitigates heterophily — GCNLayer implements learned heterophily gate
hypothesis: Iterative bidirectional querying improves accuracy over one-directional consolidation — ablation runner supports this test (ablation 1: no_iterate)
Domain-specific spatio-temporal KG objects improve temporal reasoning — ablation runner supports this (ablation 3: generic_kg)
Semantic ML layer provides generalization that KG alone cannot — ablation runner supports this (ablation 2: kg_only)
LLM-based reconciliation improves over confidence_max — ablation runner supports this (ablation 4: confidence_max)
Prototype slot count 64 is optimal for agricultural disease patterns — sweep ablation supports this (slots_8, slots_32, slots_128)
TODO_unverified: External baselines not run (Zep, Mem0, Letta, AOI) — requires external system integration
Latency-accuracy tradeoff curve for iterative cycles — profiling.py measures latency, accuracy impact from ablation runner, but combined Pareto frontier not yet plotted
Memory footprint at 50K facts — KG and semantic model memory profiled separately, not combined at target scale
Semantic Density Robustness sweep (rho 0.1-0.9) — benchmark implemented but uses small config; needs full-scale run
GCN → MLP encoder ablation — registered in ablation_runner but SemanticPatternExtractor does not support MLP encoder_type natively
Agricultural KG bootstrapping feasibility — not evaluated (requires ontology integration)

evidence: Iterative bidirectional querying protocol is implemented and testable via ablation (no_iterate)
Domain-specific spatio-temporal KG objects (CropCycle, DiseaseEvent, TreatmentAction) are first-class data model entities
CLS bicameral separation with explicit fast-write (KG) vs. slow-consolidation (semantic ML) is architecturally implemented
Research contract correctly identifies that AOI and All-Mem consolidate one-directionally; this architecture adds bidirectional iteration
concerns: The CLS bicameral pattern itself is not novel (AOI, All-Mem, Dual-System Memory all predate); novelty rests on iterative querying protocol and domain-tailored constructs
Zep/Mem0 framing as 'monolithic' was identified as factually incorrect by research analysis — architecture comparison must avoid strawman
No external baseline comparison has been run, so 'outperforms monoliths' claim remains unverified
The iterative bidirectional protocol is implemented but its effectiveness has not been empirically demonstrated over one-directional consolidation

evidence: 9 domain-specific benchmarks covering fact recall, temporal reasoning, density robustness, write throughput, expressiveness, oversmoothing, crop cycle memory, disease progression, spatial proximity
8 ablation configs covering all primary novelty claims from research contract
Semantic Density Robustness benchmark (novel metric from research contract) is implemented
Gradient flow tests, shape tests, numerical stability tests, permutation invariance tests all present
Working memory eviction, dedup cache, interface contract tests cover edge cases
Standard pytest suite has 30+ test cases across 7 test classes
concerns: External baseline comparison (Zep, Mem0, Letta, AOI) is absent — these require integration work beyond current scope
Counterfactual reasoning benchmark is referenced in research contract but not explicitly benchmarked (covered implicitly by fact_recall and temporal_reasoning)
Storage footprint at 50K facts is not measured (profiling runs at smaller scales)
Latency-accuracy Pareto curve for iterative cycles not yet plotted (individual metrics exist in ablation and profiling)

evidence: Stability Gap theorem (arXiv:2601.15313) directly motivates bicameral separation — cited in architecture rationale
GCN with heterophily gate is theoretically motivated for agricultural disease KGs where healthy/infected neighbors must remain distinct
Prototypical networks for few-shot adaptation is grounded in Snell et al. (NeurIPS 2017)
Contrastive loss for consolidation is standard for representation learning
Temporal + spatial dual indexing is motivated by agricultural query patterns
Edge-feature-aware message passing is theoretically justified for heterophilic graphs
concerns: No formal analysis of convergence for the iterative bidirectional protocol — cycles are bounded by max_iterative_cycles, not by a convergence guarantee
Expressiveness ceiling (1-WL for property graphs) is acknowledged but not proven that GCN + prototype attention exceeds it
Text embedding in semantic layer uses simple bag-of-ngrams hash; no theoretical justification that this is sufficient for matching agricultural queries to prototypes

evidence: Pytest suite runs and validates shape correctness, gradient flow, and numerical stability
Ablation runner produces accuracy and latency metrics for comparison across configs
Profiling script measures latency, FLOPs, and memory for each subsystem
concerns: No actual experimental results exist yet — tests validate code correctness, not research hypotheses
Ablation results are generated by running the ablation_runner, which requires a full training run
No comparison against any external baseline (Zep, Mem0, etc.) has been performed
Statistical significance of results is not addressed (single seed, small sample sizes)
No learning curves, convergence plots, or confidence intervals are computed

evidence: Seeded random initialization (torch.manual_seed) ensures deterministic config instantiation
All configs are frozen dataclasses (immutable after construction)
CLI-driven ablation runner and benchmark scripts enable exact reproduction
Smoke test validates end-to-end workflow before any benchmarking
Comprehensive pytest suite validates unit-level correctness before system-level evaluation
Device-portable code (CPU/CUDA tested in smoke_test.py test_device_portability)
concerns: LLM stub in controller.py returns deterministic responses — production runs with real LLMs will have non-deterministic results
Several benchmarks rely on random event generation without saving/loading the seed data
KG node/edge indexing uses id() in node_id generation, which is not deterministic across process restarts
No Dockerfile or environment specification beyond torch and Python stdlib

evidence: Shape comments are present throughout layers.py, semantic.py, kg.py (verified by smoke test test_tensor_shape_comments)
Architecture blueprint has comprehensive pseudocode with docstrings
All test classes have descriptive names and docstrings
Research contract provides clearly stated claims with evidence status
concerns: No paper draft, abstract, or figure generation is implemented
Benchmark results are printed to stdout but not formatted as publication-ready tables
No README explaining how to run the full evaluation pipeline end-to-end
Provenance tracking is implemented but provenance visualization is not

blocking_gaps.description: No external baselines (Zep, Mem0, Letta, AOI) have been evaluated. The primary claim — that this architecture outperforms monolithic models — cannot be verified without comparison runs.
The core novelty claim (iterative bidirectional querying improves accuracy) has the infrastructure to test (ablation_runner.py --ablation no_iterate) but no results have been generated.
Benchmark script (benchmarks.py) relies on random data generation. For publication-ready results, the synthetic data must be versioned and reproducible.
GCN → MLP encoder ablation (ablation 8) is registered in the ablation runner but SemanticPatternExtractor does not support encoder_type='mlp' natively — the ablation will fail at runtime.
The iterative bidirectional querying protocol is implemented but its marginal benefit over one-directional consolidation is unmeasured. This is the primary novelty claim.

bugs_found_during_validation.description: Mixed-dtype error when model is in bf16/fp16 mode: `self.norm(out.float())` converts activation to float32 but norm weights are bf16 (from `.bfloat16()` conversion), causing 'mixed dtype (CPU)' RuntimeError.
bugs_found_during_validation.fix: Replaced `self.norm(out.float())` with `F.layer_norm(out.float(), ..., weight.float(), bias.float(), ...)` — explicitly casting norm params ensures dtype consistency regardless of model dtype.

bugs_found_during_validation.description: `batch.node_mask.float()` promotes h_out from bf16 to float32 unconditionally, causing dtype mismatch between float32 h_out and downstream bf16 linear layer weights.
bugs_found_during_validation.fix: Changed `.float()` to `.to(dtype=h_out.dtype)` so the mask matches the activation dtype.

bugs_found_during_validation.description: When all labels are the same class, `neg_mask` is all-False, so `sim[neg_mask]` is a 0-element tensor and `.mean()` returns NaN.
bugs_found_during_validation.fix: Added `if neg_sim.numel() > 0:` guard around margin loss computation.

bugs_found_during_validation.location: coder/layers.py:PrototypeAttention.forward — float32 casting with bf16 attention weights
bugs_found_during_validation.description: `self.attention(query.float(), prototypes.float())` casts inputs to float32 but the attention module's `in_proj_weight` is bf16 (from `.bfloat16()` on parent model), causing 'mat1 and mat2 must have the same dtype' RuntimeError.
bugs_found_during_validation.fix: Added dtype check: if attention weights are not float32, temporarily run attention in float32, then restore original dtype.

bugs_found_during_validation.location: coder/semantic.py:190-191 — confidence_head float32 cast with bf16 weights
bugs_found_during_validation.description: `pattern_embed.float()` creates float32 tensor, but `self.confidence_head` weights are bf16 (from `.bfloat16()` on parent model), causing dtype mismatch in `F.linear`.
bugs_found_during_validation.fix: Same pattern as PrototypeAttention fix: temporarily promote confidence_head to float32 when its weights are not float32.

recommended_next_experiments.experiment: Run ablation_runner.py --all to generate accuracy × latency Pareto data across all config variants
recommended_next_experiments.rationale: This is the minimum bar for validating the architecture's design choices

recommended_next_experiments.experiment: Implement and run external baseline comparison: integrate Zep/Graphiti or Mem0 API on same agricultural benchmark
recommended_next_experiments.rationale: Without external baselines, the 'outperforms monoliths' claim cannot be evaluated

recommended_next_experiments.experiment: Run semantic_density_robustness benchmark at full scale (rho sweep 0.1-0.9, N=50+ facts) with seed-fixed data
recommended_next_experiments.rationale: This is the novel metric that directly tests the Stability Gap hypothesis in agriculture

recommended_next_experiments.experiment: Plot iterative cycle count vs. accuracy tradeoff: sweep max_iterative_cycles={1,2,3,5} at fixed event count
recommended_next_experiments.rationale: Determines the optimal iteration count for the latency budget (target: p99 < 5s)

recommended_next_experiments.experiment: Profile storage footprint at 50K facts (target: < 500 MB as per architecture blueprint)
recommended_next_experiments.rationale: Deployment requirement; not yet measured at target scale

recommended_next_experiments.experiment: Implement and validate the GCN→MLP encoder ablation by adding MLP encoder support to SemanticPatternExtractor
recommended_next_experiments.rationale: Validates whether graph structure awareness provides measurable benefit

recommended_next_experiments.experiment: Run prototype slot utilization entropy analysis after consolidation to detect oversmoothing
recommended_next_experiments.rationale: Risk mitigation for the over-smoothing concern raised in architecture blueprint §10
